input.background: Table 14.3 shows calculation of the correlation coefficient for the illustrative data. The correlation coefficient $r=0.737$ represents a strong positive correlation.
input.question: What does the correlation coefficient $r=0.737$ represent?

Country.description: The names of the countries included in the dataset.

x.description: The independent variable representing a numerical measure (e.g., income, production, etc.) for each country.

z_x.description: The standardized values of x calculated as (x_i - mean_x) / std_x.

y.description: The dependent variable representing a numerical measure (e.g., consumption, output, etc.) for each country.

z_y.description: The standardized values of y calculated as (y_i - mean_y) / std_y.

z_x_prime_z_y.description: The product of standardized values z_x and z_y for each country.

mean_x.description: The mean value of the variable x.

std_x.description: The standard deviation of the variable x.

mean_y.description: The mean value of the variable y.

std_y.description: The standard deviation of the variable y.

r.description: The correlation coefficient representing the strength and direction of the linear relationship between x and y.
